free_text: NEUTRON SCATTERING CROSS SECTIONS FROM 14-N AND
TIME RESOLUTION WAS MONITORED WITH A ZERO DEGREE
THIRD DETECTOR AT 55 DEG ABOVE THE REACTION PLANE.
DURING EACH ANGULAR DISTRIBUTION RUN SPECTRA WERE TAKEN
WITH POLYETHYLENE AND CARBON AT 30 DEGREES TO
SEPARATE THE CARBON AND HYDROGEN PEAKS.
YIELDS WERE OBTAINED FROM THE SPECTRA BY SUMMING GATES
SET ON THE GROUPS OF INTEREST-ELASTIC AND INELASTIC
MONTE CARLO ANALYSIS WAS DONE TO ESTIMATE THE FINITE
COMPRESSED BE(3)N(2) POWDER IN A 2.59 CM HIGH AND
0.939 CM RADIUS STAINLESS STEEL CIRCULAR CYLINDER.
